summary: The Docker-based reproduction compiles the exact vulnerable OpenWire parser from the pinned Artemis 2.44.0 revision, sends only the oversized 4-byte frame length over TCP, and triggers OutOfMemoryError in OpenWireFrameParser.decode.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should compile the exact vulnerable OpenWire parser from the pinned repository revision, send only an oversized 4-byte length header over TCP, and observe an OutOfMemoryError originating from OpenWireFrameParser.decode.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned commit 437105493d75b6156ba7bb1f77bae93de2182e30, built the Docker image, compiled the exact parser source from the mounted checkout, and printed EVOHUNT_OPENWIRE_OOM_CONFIRMED with an OutOfMemoryError stack trace rooted in OpenWireFrameParser.decode.
verification_method: verify.sh requires the exact proof string EVOHUNT_OPENWIRE_OOM_CONFIRMED and a stack trace containing OpenWireFrameParser.decode before reporting success.
evidence_produced_by_run: The packaged rerun printed EVOHUNT_OPENWIRE_OOM_CONFIRMED after sending only the oversized length header.
The reproduction finished through ./reproduction/run.sh and ended with [evohunt] SUCCESS: reproduction completed and verified.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
